frames.name: Cuban Self-Defense Frame
frames.description: Presents the incident as Cuban forces defending territorial sovereignty against armed infiltrators who fired first
frames.emphasis: Cuban territorial sovereignty, self-defense response, armed infiltration attempt

frames.name: Anti-Communist Accountability Frame
frames.description: Frames the incident as Cuban communist aggression requiring investigation and accountability
frames.emphasis: Cuban government untrustworthiness, need for investigation, communist regime criticism

frames.name: Factual Investigation Frame
frames.description: Emphasizes the need for independent verification and investigation before drawing conclusions
frames.emphasis: Independent verification, gathering facts, avoiding speculation

frames.name: Exile Terrorism Frame
frames.description: Presents the boat occupants as Cuban exiles planning terrorist infiltration with criminal backgrounds
frames.emphasis: Terrorist planning, criminal histories, exile group activities

frames.name: Family Rescue Frame
frames.description: Suggests the boat was part of efforts to evacuate relatives from Cuba
frames.emphasis: Humanitarian mission to help family members escape Cuba

outlierPerspectives: Historical parallel to 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown (The Guardian, BBC)
Connection to current oil embargo and humanitarian crisis context (The Guardian)
Specific identification of wanted individuals with terrorist connections (BBC, The Guardian)
Mention of confession by alleged accomplice on Cuban soil (BBC, Breitbart)
missingContext: Independent verification of Cuban claims about weapons and equipment found
Confirmation of identities and citizenship status of those involved
Details about the boat's actual mission and departure point
U.S. government's official position beyond Rubio's preliminary statements
International maritime law regarding territorial waters and self-defense
Previous incidents' outcomes and investigations in similar cases
